Patch Set 3:

Hi James, Please review. Thanks

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Arshad – I apologize for the delay. 

I’ve looked at this patch several times and had a couple of issues with it. I’m uncomfortable with the modification to get_version() that looks specifically for the file name in test 0a and takes specific action based on that name. I also don’t think it’s the job of get_version() to check if there is a valid output and return an error, but this point could be debated. 

I’m not totally against this patch, I think the purpose of test 0a is still intact and the changes you made to get_version() are the least intrusive compared to modifying all calls to get_version() to check the return status. Why can’t we add a return code to get_version() is there is no output and the subtest can choose to check the return code or not?

Please let me know what you think.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

Hi James,
Thanks for the review.

>I also don’t think it’s the job of get_version() to check if there is >a valid output and return an error, but this point could be debated. 
The idea was to do minimal changes to existing code. And considering that get_version() was not part of library and used widely I added the check. I agree with you get_version() should not selectively validate output. 
>Why can’t we add a return code to get_version() is there is no output >and the subtest can choose to check the return code or not?
I have added a wrapper function to take care of this.  Please comment if this is what you are looking out for ?
Thanks

parents.subject: LU-9558 tests: add completion.h header to kinode.c

commit.subject: LU-9379 tests: Improve get_version to handle empty return value
commit.message:
LU-9379 tests: Improve get_version to handle empty return value

replay-vbr fails when get_version() returns empty value to
either pre/post variable. This patch modifies replay-vbr
get_version() by returning '-1' in case getobjversion fails.

This patch also adds chk_get_version(). Which is a wrapper
to get_version(). chk_get_version() helps in verifying
return code of get_version().

Signed-off-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Jadhav Vikram <jadhav.vikram@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Ujjwal Lanjewar <ujjwal.lanjewar@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Parinay Kondekar <parinay.kondekar@seagate.com>

Reviewed-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
